title: [Design Doc] New feature to import local CA certs
body:
Related to https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-3839

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Thank you @TomSweeneyRedHat, I took all your suggestions 👍

@benoitf @slemeur your review of the design document for this feature is important to verify that Podman Desktop needs are covered. Especially to confirm that:
- The Podman Desktop use case is covered in the ["Use cases" section](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/a4781ff971ec12ef6abe1ea0608662ec25824ba4/contrib/design-docs/import-ca-certs.md#use-cases)
- It's ok ~~to trigger the import of the certificates only with [`podman machine start`](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/a4781ff971ec12ef6abe1ea0608662ec25824ba4/contrib/design-docs/import-ca-certs.md#introducing-a-new-machine-start-flag) (and not with `podman machine init --now` for instance)~~ that
   - the option is set at the initialization of a machine (`podman machine init --import-native-ca`)
   - can be changed afterwards with `podman machine set --import-native-ca=[true|false]` (requires a restart).
- The default to **NOT** import the certificates at machine start is ok

UPDATE: I changed this comment to reflect the latest changes to the design document.

On last little nit @l0rd .  Apologies if I missed it, or worse yet, caused it with my suggestion.
Otherwise
LGTM
@TomSweeneyRedHat, thank you. I have also applied your last suggestion.

> Is there a reason not to enable this by default? It seems like sensible behavior.

Yes, I agree. We can do that. But considered that it may impact on the startup time of the machine we may want to be conservative, and eventually turn it on by default after a few releases.

Can you elaborate more on why a mount is not more ideal in this manner.  One of my chief concerns is the time added to copy these files.  Every second that takes, is added to the "boot time' (as defined by the time it takes to return the prompt to the user.).  I was, in fact, hoping we could remove some of the things we do at startup now because our boot times, imho are becoming noticable.

Thank you @baude for looking at the design document. I appreciate your feedback. I have considered the copy simpler compared to mounting because it doesn't require extra machine configuration. From performances point of view, from my tests the copy takes a few tens of ms for a file of ~100 certs. And for some providers, mounting the file requires a network transfer too. So this is basically my rational. However you have much more experience with machines and it's absolutely possible that I have missed something. If you and @mheon think it's better to go with a mount of these certificate files I will be happy to update the doc.

> @benoitf @slemeur your review of the design document for this feature is important to verify that Podman Desktop needs are covered. Especially to confirm that:
> 
>     * The Podman Desktop use case is covered in the ["Use cases" section](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/a4781ff971ec12ef6abe1ea0608662ec25824ba4/contrib/design-docs/import-ca-certs.md#use-cases)
> 
>     * It's ok to trigger the import of the certificates only with [`podman machine start`](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/a4781ff971ec12ef6abe1ea0608662ec25824ba4/contrib/design-docs/import-ca-certs.md#introducing-a-new-machine-start-flag) (and not with `podman machine init --now` for instance).
> 
>     * The default to **NOT** import the certificates at machine start is ok

Podman Desktop uses `podman machine init --now` if user creates a machine and wants it started (the default) so we should make sure the certificates are copied if the flag `import-native-ca` is set

> > @benoitf @slemeur your review of the design document for this feature is important to verify that Podman Desktop needs are covered. Especially to confirm that:
> > ```
> > * The Podman Desktop use case is covered in the ["Use cases" section](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/a4781ff971ec12ef6abe1ea0608662ec25824ba4/contrib/design-docs/import-ca-certs.md#use-cases)
> > 
> > * It's ok to trigger the import of the certificates only with [`podman machine start`](https://github.com/l0rd/podman/blob/a4781ff971ec12ef6abe1ea0608662ec25824ba4/contrib/design-docs/import-ca-certs.md#introducing-a-new-machine-start-flag) (and not with `podman machine init --now` for instance).
> > 
> > * The default to **NOT** import the certificates at machine start is ok
> > ```
> 
> Podman Desktop uses `podman machine init --now` if user creates a machine and wants it started (the default) so we should make sure the certificates are copied if the flag `import-native-ca` is set

Thank you for the feedback @jeffmaury. I wasn't sure about that. The design document has been updated to make it a `machine init` option. With `podman machine init --now --import-native-ca`, the certificates will be imported automatically.

I have done [some investigation](https://gist.github.com/l0rd/6e9b6640cc8ec780b53d2a74d9400936) on the certificates file mounting vs transmitting via SCP. 

| PROVIDER | RUN TYPE | STARTUP TIME (MEAN) |
| ---- | ---- | ---- |
| WSL | no import | 6.2s |
| | mnt | +3.9s |
| | scp | +5.1s |
| HYPERV | no import | 22.3s |
| | mnt | +3.4s |
| | scp | +4.7s |
| APPLEHV | no import | 10.0s |
| | mnt | +1.4s |
| | scp | +1.6s |
| LIBKRUN | no import | 9.0s |
| | mnt | +1.3s |
| | scp | +1.4s |

Summarizing the SCP adds 1s/0.1s on Windows/macOS to the startup process.

But most of the time the certificates file is already mounted into the machine (because `$HOME` is mounted by default) hence we can avoid to use SCP to copy the file.

I have modified the design document to reflect that:

> The folder containing the `PEM` file is automatically mounted in the guest with the default machine configuration. When this is not the case, because the machine volumes have been customized for example, the file is transferred from the host to the guest, with the `SCP` protocol.

cc @mheon

Mounting SGTM, good to have numbers.
@baude Any thoughts on this one?

> Mounting SGTM, good to have numbers. @baude Any thoughts on this one?

it was always my preference to do mounting because if the certs change while the machine is active, we dont get into a situation where the user will be really confused when it breaks.

I think in general this LGTM.
We have 2 LGTM @containers/podman-maintainers. Can someone please merge?
